title: Translate vetted explainer (links to ewing-info-translations)
context: Backlog — ONLY post-M5, dual-expert per language. Translate the vetted, expert-signed-off explainer; high risk because it is patient-facing oncology content and each language needs its own dual-expert sign-off. Coordinates with the ewing-info-translations project. Binding cancer guardrails apply: ONLY open-access / aggregate / de-identified public data; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; not medical advice; when in doubt, stop and surface. No partner/beneficiary is secured yet, so verifiedNeed is false.
objective: Translate the vetted explainer into a target language (one representative task), with per-language dual-expert review, preserving the 'not medical advice' framing.
acceptanceCriteria: Translation begins ONLY after the M5 explainer is published with oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off.
Each translated language carries its own dual-expert (oncologist + patient-advocate) sign-off; veto = block.
Preserves 'not medical advice' framing; no prognosis/treatment/patient-specific guidance; source license respected.
Languages are NOT pre-enumerated here — they expand on advocacy-partner/scope confirmation (see fan-out note).
outputLicense: CC-BY-4.0 (source-compatible; the vetted explainer is CC-BY-4.0 — never relicense copyrighted source)
